Rubus radula is a type of bramble plant, specifically a thorny shrub that belongs to the Rosaceae family. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its health benefits, which include anti-inflammatory and antioxidant properties that may help to reduce symptoms of digestive issues and protect against oxidative stress. The bioactive constituents of Rubus radula include flavonoids, phenolic acids, and alkaloids, which are responsible for its medicinal properties. The most common herbal preparations made with Rubus radula include teas, infusions, and tinctures, which are often used to support digestive health and reduce inflammation.
